title: Bill Belichick gets candid on Tom Brady’s growth with Patriots: ‘He wasn’t even really good, but he became great’
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Bill Belichick discussed Tom Brady's transformation from a fourth-string quarterback to a five-time Super Bowl MVP on 'Pardon My Take.'
Belichick emphasized Brady's commitment and gradual development, contributing to his eventual success as a key figure in Patriots' victories.
Belichick, now coaching at University of North Carolina, reflected on differences from NFL and shared his dislike for coffee on the podcast.

title: As Minogue pours millions into his own campaign, state lawmaker pushes to ban self-funding
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: GOP candidate Mike Minogue has self-funded his gubernatorial campaign with $13.5 million from his own wealth.
State Sen. Rebecca Rausch proposed a state budget amendment to limit unlimited self-funding in political campaigns.
Massachusetts allows individuals to donate up to $1,000 annually to campaigns, excluding self-funding limits for candidates.

title: Celtics fans voted NBA’s ‘most obnoxious’ in anonymous player poll
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Celtics fans were voted the NBA's 'most obnoxious' by 161 players in an anonymous player poll by The Athletic.
TD Garden was chosen as having the most passionate fans, narrowly beating Madison Square Garden with a 20.3% vote.
Celtics fans received 32% of votes for being the most obnoxious, ahead of Philadelphia's Xfinity Mobile Arena's 17.9%.

title: Stephen A. Smith responds to Jaylen Brown’s comments: ‘Be careful what you wish for’
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Stephen A. Smith responded to Jaylen Brown's criticism, warning him to 'be careful what you wish for.'
Brown called Smith the 'face of clickbait media' and suggested he retire during a Twitch stream.
Smith addressed Brown's remarks on 'First Take', emphasizing his comments were not meant as disrespect.

title: FAA awarding Logan Airport $2.8 million for family-friendly upgrades
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Logan Airport receives $2.8 million from the FAA for Kidport renovations in Terminals A, B, and C.
The funding is part of a $970 million national initiative to enhance family-friendly airport facilities.
The renovations aim to provide engaging play areas for children, improving travel experiences for families.

title: Beverly mansion robbery suspect indicted on 10 charges
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Emajae Brown, 25, was indicted on 10 charges in Essex Superior Court for a Beverly mansion robbery in March.
Brown allegedly stole valuables worth nearly $8 million from 'Rock Edge' and assaulted a housekeeper during the robbery.
Authorities identified Brown using surveillance technology and arrested him with over $300,000 in cash and gold from the mansion.

title: Boston police commissioner questions previous sentence for alleged Cambridge shooter
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Boston Police Commissioner Michael Cox criticized the lenient sentence for alleged Cambridge shooter Tyler Brown.
Tyler Brown's 2020 sentence was five to six years for shooting at police, against the requested 10 to 12 years.
Brown allegedly fired an assault-style rifle on Memorial Drive, injuring two, nearly six years after his South End offense.

title: Should Boston host the 2028 Democratic National Convention?
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Boston is a finalist to host the 2028 Democratic National Convention, competing with Atlanta, Chicago, Denver, and Philadelphia.
Hosting the convention could bring approximately $400 million in economic benefits to Boston, according to city officials.
Boston leaders, including Mayor Michelle Wu, emphasize the city’s historical ties to American democracy and major event experience.

title: Early thoughts on the Celtics’ offseason: Red flags, silver linings, and biggest needs
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Celtics face offseason after blowing a 3-1 playoff lead for the first time in franchise history.
Team showed regular-season success with 56 wins but struggled in playoffs, especially with 3-point reliance.
Key offseason needs include adding a scoring big man and a veteran two-guard to boost offensive depth.

title: 21-year-old woman charged after wrong-way crash with N.H. State Police cruiser
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Cassandra Aldecoa, 21, was arrested after driving the wrong way on Route 101 in Exeter, New Hampshire, colliding with a state trooper.
Aldecoa faces charges including reckless conduct, second-degree assault, and DUI; passenger Zachary Lapierre faces disorderly conduct charges.
Both were arraigned in Brentwood District Court; Aldecoa is in custody, and Lapierre was released on personal recognizance.
